input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A blue and silver vacuum head advances across a beige carpet, moving from the foreground toward the center then retreating upper right. The silver wand stretches out as a dark handle structure enters the lower left foreground. [4-8] The head is pulled back to center, angling the wand downward while a large black handle dominates the immediate foreground. The handle vanishes, re-centering the wand as the head shifts slightly right and forward. [8-12] The blue head pushes forward along a carpet boundary, transitioning from darker/coarser left to lighter/smoother right. The silver wand guides the device steadily away until a dark rectangular furniture piece appears in the upper right corner near the wall.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The blue vacuum head moves forward across the beige carpet toward the upper center. A black plastic handle grip appears in the lower foreground, attached to the silver wand extending upward. The dark base of a piece of furniture stays visible in the upper right corner as the cleaning tool advances away from the viewpoint.

[14-16] A black handle grip remains visible in the foreground, attached to the silver wand. The blue vacuum head stays centrally on the beige carpet. The dark furniture base in the upper right corner persists within the frame as the device continues across the speckled surface.

[16-18] The black handle grip disappears from the lower foreground, leaving the silver wand extending toward the blue vacuum head centered on the beige carpet. The cleaning tool angles slightly right across the textured surface. A dark furniture base or curtain remains visible in the upper right corner as the vacuum head stays positioned on the floor.

[18-20] A black handle grip emerges in the lower left foreground, connecting to the silver wand extending toward the blue vacuum head. The device continues moving across the beige carpet, with the head positioned further away on the textured floor surface as the cleaning motion persists.

[20-22] The black handle grip previously seen in the lower left foreground vanishes from view, isolating the silver metal wand extending toward the blue vacuum head. The cleaning tool stays centered on the beige carpet, with the rectangular head resting flat against the textured floor surface near the middle of the frame.

[22-24] The blue and silver vacuum head remains positioned centrally on the beige carpet, connected to the silver wand extending toward the bottom left. No black handle grip is visible in the immediate foreground. The device rests on the textured floor, adjacent to a vertical strip where the carpet appears noticeably lighter and cleaner on the left side.

samples.output: <think>The blue vacuum head moves leftward across the beige carpet. A black plastic handle grip enters the lower left foreground, attached to the silver wand. The wand angles toward the bottom left corner as the device is manipulated, bringing the handle structure into view against the textured floor surface.</think><action>silent</action>

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A blue and silver vacuum head advances across a beige carpet, moving from the foreground toward the center then retreating upper right. [4-8] The head is pulled back to center, angling the wand downward while a large black handle dominates the immediate foreground. The handle vanishes, re-centering the wand as the head shifts slightly right and forward. [8-12] The blue head pushes forward along a carpet boundary, transitioning from darker/coarser left to lighter/smoother right. The silver wand guides the device steadily away until a dark rectangular furniture piece appears in the upper right corner near the wall.

[12-26] A blue rectangular vacuum head attached to a silver metal wand moves across a textured beige carpet. Initially, a black plastic handle grip is visible in the lower foreground as the device advances toward the upper center, passing a dark furniture base in the top right. The handle grip intermittently disappears and reappears in the lower left as the wand angles and the head shifts position. By 22s, a vertical strip of noticeably lighter, cleaner carpet is visible on the left side next to the vacuum head. The sequence ends with the black handle re-entering the lower left foreground as the head moves leftward across the floor.

[26-28] A blue rectangular vacuum head rests centrally on a beige carpet, attached to a silver metal wand angling toward the bottom left, with a black handle grip visible in the immediate foreground and held steady. [28-32] The black handle grip disappears, but the blue head and silver wand maintain a steady position over textured fibers without significant lateral movement. [32-38] The black handle grip re-enters the lower left foreground and remains visible. Throughout, the device holds a consistent orientation on the floor, exhibiting minimal displacement and creating no new cleaning paths on the carpet.

[38-42] The black handle grip is absent from the lower foreground as the blue vacuum head remains centered on the beige carpet, attached to a silver wand. A bright patch of sunlight illuminates the upper left floor near a curtain edge, highlighting textured fibers.
[42-46] The device angles toward the lower left before the blue rectangular head pushes forward, extending the silver wand away. A vertical strip of lighter, cleaned carpet fibers becomes visible to the left, emphasized by the sunlight.
[46-50] The vacuum head angles slightly rightward and then holds a stationary position on the beige carpet. The silver wand extends toward the bottom left while the black handle remains out of view. Sunlight continues to illuminate the upper left corner as the device maintains its placement.

Sunlight persists in the upper left corner, highlighting the carpet texture near the curtain. The device maintains a steady position with no significant displacement across the floor.

[52-54] A black handle structure reappears in the lower left foreground, obscuring the silver wand connected to the central blue vacuum head. The device rests on textured beige carpet illuminated by sunlight on the left. The handle vanishes from view, leaving the wand and head stationary against the floor fibers.

[54-56] The camera pans upward, removing the vacuum cleaner from view and revealing a black furniture base with white stitching. The silver wand and blue head disappear. The frame centers on the object resting on the beige carpet near a wall corner, shifting focus from the cleaning tool to the room's furnishings.

queries.question: Has the vacuum cleaner stopped cleaning after previously moving across the carpet?

   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
